summary: The July 8 scout emphasizes rigorous relativistic-hydrodynamics validity criteria, lattice-constrained open-quantum-system bottomonium transport, vector-meson spin-alignment phenomenology, a sPHENIX p+p isolated-prompt-photon baseline, and AI/ML methods with credible value for collider-analysis reproducibility, PDE emulation, nuclear-data modeling, and nuclear DFT.
keywords: Bayesian neural networks for nuclear data
top_takeaway: arXiv:2607.05639 appears to be the strongest methodological contribution because it gives broad nonlinear causality and well-posedness criteria for relativistic viscous hydrodynamics, a core tool for heavy-ion modeling.

papers.title: Nonlinear Causality and Strong Hyperbolicity of Einstein-Israel-Stewart Theories of Transient Relativistic Fluid Dynamics
papers.authors_short: Ian Cordeiro, Enrico Speranza, Fabio S. Bemfica, Marcelo M. Disconzi, Jorge Noronha
papers.abstract: Derives nonlinear causality criteria and sufficient strong-hyperbolicity conditions for broad transient relativistic viscous-fluid theories, with direct relevance to heavy-ion hydrodynamic modeling.

papers.title: Bottomonium production in an open quantum system approach with interactions from lattice quantum chromodynamic
papers.abstract: Uses a Lindblad open-quantum-system framework with lattice-constrained in-medium heavy-quark potentials to study Upsilon nuclear modification factors in Pb-Pb collisions.

papers.title: Measurement of isolated prompt photon production in p+p collisions at sqrt(s) = 200 GeV with the sPHENIX detector
papers.authors_short: sPHENIX Collaboration: M. I. Abdulhamid, U. Acharya, G. Adawi, I. Ahmed, C. A. Aidala, et al.
papers.abstract: Measures isolated prompt-photon production in 2024 p+p data at RHIC and compares the cross section with PYTHIA and NLO/NNLO pQCD, establishing a baseline for future sPHENIX heavy-ion photon measurements.

papers.title: Vector-Meson Spin Alignment from Anisotropic Quark or Hadron Coalescence
papers.authors_short: Wen-Bo Dong, Xin-Li Sheng, Yi-Liang Yin, Dirk H. Rischke, Qun Wang
papers.abstract: Studies how early anisotropy can induce spin alignment of phi and K*0 mesons through quark or pseudoscalar-meson coalescence, with sign differences proposed as production-mechanism diagnostics.

papers.title: Quantum decoherence: a study applied to quarkonium-like bound states in strongly interacting matter
papers.authors_short: Gabriele Coci, Salvatore Plumari, Giuseppe Falci
papers.abstract: Models a quarkonium-like bound state coupled to a strongly interacting reservoir and studies coherence loss under static and fireball-like time-dependent couplings.

papers.title: Articulating Assumptions in AI-Generated Scientific Analyses through Task Decomposition
papers.abstract: Introduces a multi-agent quantity-grounded semantic differencing workflow for validating AI-generated scientific analysis code, with validation on representative collider-physics analyses.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
papers.authors_short: Raul Jimenez, Svitlana Mayboroda, Pavlos Protopapas, Leonid Sarieddine, David N. Spergel, Pedro Tarancon-Alvarez
papers.abstract: Uses multihead PINNs to learn low-dimensional latent manifolds for PDE solution families, with Burgers, heat, and wave equations as tests.

papers.title: Evaluation of U-235 and U-238 Fission Product Yields Using Bayesian Neural Networks: Comparison of Baseline and Physics-Informed Models
papers.authors_short: Chun-Yuan Qiao, Ya-Xuan Wang, Jun-Chen Pei, Chun-Wang Ma, Yong-Jing Chen, et al.
papers.abstract: Compares baseline and physics-informed Bayesian neural networks for U-235 and U-238 fission-yield evaluation, reporting improved reconstruction and narrower confidence intervals with physics descriptors.

papers.title: ML and AI for density functional theory: different priorities for Kohn-Sham and orbital-free DFT, for electronic and nuclear DFT
papers.abstract: Reviews how ML priorities differ across Kohn-Sham, orbital-free, electronic, and nuclear DFT, highlighting symbolic-regression opportunities for kinetic-energy functionals.

papers.title: Relativistic Vorticity in the Quark-Gluon Plasma: Generation Symmetries, Explosive Dilution, and Azimuthal Spin Alignment
papers.authors_short: Malak Ait Tamlihat, Ghizlane Ez-Zobayr, Laurent Schoeffel, Yahya Tayalati
papers.abstract: Discusses QGP vorticity, geometric dilution, and azimuthal spin-alignment signatures, but the claims appear broader than the abstract-level validation supports.

papers.title: Relativistic Hydrodynamics and Vorticity Dynamics in High-Energy Heavy-Ion Collisions: A Collective Flow Perspective
papers.authors_short: Malak Ait Tamlihat, Ghizlane Ez-Zobayr, Laurent Schoeffel, Yahya Tayalati
papers.abstract: Provides an overview of hydrodynamics, vorticity transport, and spin observables, but overlaps thematically with arXiv:2607.03716 and should be read cautiously until validation is clearer.

archive_notes.limitation: The configured GitHub app could create new archive files, but the safe replacement path for existing index.html required a base tree SHA that was not exposed by the available app reads; attempting to use the latest commit SHA as base_tree_sha was rejected by GitHub as invalid tree info.
